╭──────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ >_ OpenAI Codex (v0.130.0)                   │
│                                              │
│ model:     gpt-5.5 medium   /model to change │
│ directory: ~/Desktop/codex-terminal-manager  │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────╯

  Tip: New Use /fast to enable our fastest inference with increased plan usage.


› Smoke test worker. Do not edit files. Wait for manager instructions and
  report status only.


• Working (5s • esc to interrupt)


› Improve documentation in @filename

  gpt-5.5 medium · ~/Desktop/codex-terminal-manager · qa-live-smoke-readiness

smoke-20260519052854 "..."`;
    the legacy `nudge` command is only for old file-backed workers and should
  not
    be the first choice for session-bound pairs.
  - If worker progress reveals new edge cases, tests, polish, or scope
    boundaries, ask the worker to propose must-have vs follow-up criteria.
  - After a worker proposes separated criteria, use `scripts/workerctl
  criteria-plan`
    to draft reviewed criteria commands, then run only the commands you agree
  with.
  - Record useful criteria with `scripts/workerctl criteria`.
    Examples:
    - `scripts/workerctl criteria codex-smoke-task-smoke-20260519052854 --add
  --criterion "..." --source worker_proposed --status proposed`
    - `scripts/workerctl criteria codex-smoke-task-smoke-20260519052854 --add
  --criterion "..." --source manager_inferred --status accepted`
    - `criterion_id=$(scripts/workerctl criteria codex-smoke-task-smoke-
  20260519052854 --add --criterion "..." --source worker_proposed --status
  proposed | python3 -c 'import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)
  ["affected_criterion"]["id"])')`
    - `scripts/workerctl criteria codex-smoke-task-smoke-20260519052854
  --satisfy "$criterion_id" --evidence-json
  '{"command":"...","status":"pass"}'`
  - Use `worker_proposed` for criteria that came from the worker.
  - Use `manager_inferred` for criteria inferred from manager config, cycle
    evidence, or the manager's own inspection; `manager_config` is not a valid
    criteria source.
  - Before finishing, compare worker receipts/verification against accepted
  open criteria.
  - Communicate with the worker only through workerctl session/task commands.
  - Do not edit project files unless the user explicitly asks this manager
    session to change workerctl itself.


◦ Starting MCP servers (1/3): codex_apps, posthog (2s • esc to interrupt)


› Write tests for @filename

  gpt-5.5 medium · ~/Desktop/codex-terminal-manager · qa-live-smoke-readiness
